retrieved_doc_snippets: From Here to Eternity (disambiguation) | From Here to Eternity is a 1953 film.

From Here to Eternity (miniseries) | From Here to Eternity is a 1979 American three-part, six-hour television miniseries and a remake of the 1953 film "From Here to Eternity" based on the 1951 novel of the same name. All three conclude with the December 7, 1941 attack on Pearl Harbor. The miniseries originally aired in three two-hour installments on three consecutive Wednesdays on NBC on February 14, 21 and 28, 1979.

From Here to Eternity | From Here to Eternity is a 1953 drama film directed by Fred Zinnemann and based on the novel of the same name by James Jones. The picture deals with the tribulations of three U.S. Army soldiers, played by Burt Lancaster, Montgomery Clift, and Frank Sinatra, stationed on Hawaii in the months leading up to the attack on Pearl Harbor. Deborah Kerr and Donna Reed portray the women in their lives and the supporting cast includes Ernest Borgnine, Philip Ober, Jack Warden, Micke ...[truncated]

Underworld: The Eternal War | Underworld: The Eternal War (also known as Underworld: The Game) is a 2004 third-person shooter video game based on the vampire film "Underworld" which was released in 2003.

From Here to Eternity: Live | From Here to Eternity: Live is a live album by English punk rock band The Clash. It was released on 4 October 1999 through Epic Records. The songs were recorded at different shows. Some of the recordings featured also appear in the film "Rude Boy". "London's Burning", "What's My Name" and "I Fought the Law" were instrumentally overdubbed to repair some technical deficiencies of the original live recording.

From Here to Eternity (TV series) | From Here to Eternity is a dramatic television series that aired in 1980. It is a spinoff of the 1979 miniseries of the same title. The series features most of the cast members from the original miniseries, including William Devane and Kim Basinger. Barbara Hershey replaced Natalie Wood for the role of Karen Holmes.

Deirdre Lenihan | Deirdre Lenihan (born May 19, 1946 in New York, New York, U.S.) is an American actress. She first appeared in the 1972 film "Glass Houses". She has also appeared in numerous television series, such as "From Here to Eternity", "Needles and Pins", and "The Waltons".
source_state.summary_2: I can’t determine an actress who starred in both from the supplied passages (they don’t list Third Person’s cast). If you mean Third Person (2013) and From Here to Eternity (1953), there’s no overlap in principal actresses. Please specify which versions or let me search the cast lists so I can answer definitively.
source_state.answer: No — there is no actress who starred in both Third Person (2013) and From Here to Eternity (1953). If you meant different versions of those titles, tell me which ones and I’ll check their casts.

output.query: Kim Basinger Third Person "From Here to Eternity" 1979
